# Security Checklist: Runtime Inventory and Constraints

**Purpose**: Validate that XPLAT-001 requirements and plan define supply-chain
rubric boundaries, consumer-trust evidence, and unsupported-guarantee guardrails
before tasks.
**Created**: 2026-06-25
**Feature**: [Runtime Inventory and Constraints](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based
on feature context and requirements.

## Supply-Chain Rubric Coverage

- [x] CHK001 Does the supply-chain rubric require dependency policy and lockfile discipline as explicit evaluation criteria? [Coverage, Spec SC-006, Plan Supply-Chain Rubric Scope]
- [x] CHK002 Does the supply-chain rubric require generated payload integrity as an explicit evaluation criterion and must-have concern? [Coverage, Spec FR-009, Data Model Supply-Chain Rubric]
- [x] CHK003 Does the supply-chain rubric require vulnerability scanning as an explicit evaluation criterion? [Coverage, Spec SC-006, Plan Supply-Chain Rubric Scope]
- [x] CHK004 Does the supply-chain rubric require provenance or attestation options as an explicit evaluation criterion? [Coverage, Spec SC-006, Plan Supply-Chain Rubric Scope]
- [x] CHK005 Does the supply-chain rubric require checksums or signatures as explicit evaluation evidence? [Coverage, Spec SC-006, Data Model Weighted Criteria]
- [x] CHK006 Does the supply-chain rubric require SBOM feasibility as explicit evaluation evidence? [Coverage, Spec SC-006, Data Model Weighted Criteria]
- [x] CHK007 Does the supply-chain rubric require consumer-local verification as explicit evaluation evidence? [Coverage, Spec SC-006, Plan Supply-Chain Rubric Scope]

## Trust Boundary and Non-Guarantee Controls

- [x] CHK008 Do XPLAT-001 artifacts prohibit selecting a required security model or control set before XPLAT-003? [Consistency, Spec FR-010, Research Candidate Evidence Decision]
- [x] CHK009 Do XPLAT-001 artifacts prohibit marketing unsupported native Windows or security guarantees in public docs? [Clarity, Spec FR-011, Data Model Must-Have Gates]
- [x] CHK010 Does the rubric require documentation truthfulness so later reports distinguish implemented controls from evaluation targets? [Clarity, Data Model Weighted Criteria, Plan Supply-Chain Rubric Scope]
- [x] CHK011 Are generated payload rows treated as evidence/handoff material rather than proof of implemented integrity controls? [Consistency, Spec Clarifications Session 2, Plan Phase 1 Design]

## Release-Gate Boundaries

- [x] CHK012 Do the spec and plan explicitly separate first-release must-have supply-chain gates from deferred hardening evidence so XPLAT-001 does not imply every evaluated control is required immediately? [Resolved, Completeness, Spec FR-009, Spec SC-006, Plan Supply-Chain Rubric Scope]
- [x] CHK013 Are pass/fail must-have gates separated from weighted criteria so reviewers can distinguish threshold requirements from scoring inputs? [Clarity, Spec Clarifications Session 3, Data Model Supply-Chain Rubric]
- [x] CHK014 Does the plan keep first-release security work static and source-traceable instead of adding runtime probes or native platform UAT? [Consistency, Spec FR-012, Plan Verification Plan]

## Consumer Verification and Evidence Quality

- [x] CHK015 Does the rubric require what maintainers verify before release and what consumers can verify locally after installation? [Completeness, Data Model Must-Have Gates]
- [x] CHK016 Are provenance and generated payload integrity treated as evidence targets for XPLAT-003 rather than claims already implemented by XPLAT-001? [Consistency, Spec FR-010, Plan Supply-Chain Rubric Scope]
- [x] CHK017 Are known gaps and deferred work required to name follow-up specs or issues in the PR packet? [Traceability, Spec PR Review Packet Requirements]
- [x] CHK018 Is rollback scoped to removing the report and roadmap note, with no security feature flag implied by this non-runtime phase? [Clarity, Plan Review Packet Notes]
